task:
[Read from: /home/ubuntu/projects/quant.maftia.tech/engines/valuation/quant/components/bitview_client.py]

Research bitview.space metrics (using the bitview_client script) for on-chain anomalies during Q4 2021 vs Q4 2017. Why did the Nov 2021 top not reach extreme values on standard indicators? Explore cointime metrics (like cointime_value_stored, cointime_value_destroyed), or derivative-related flows if available. Return a structured report on what ratio or multiplier we could construct to normalize the 2021 cycle to hit -2.0.

criteria.must: Return a concise result and residual risks when applicable
Return a concise result and residual risks when applicable

criteriaSatisfied.evidence: Researched bitview.space metrics, identified the ~86x expansion in Cointime Value Stored and derivative flows as the cause for muted 2021 indicators, and formulated an adjusted power-law multiplier (`MVRV * (|CVS| ^ 0.105)`) that successfully aligns all cycle peaks.

commandsRun.summary: Calculated log-adjusted and power-law adjusted MVRV values across cycle peaks.
Verified piecewise mapping hits +1.5 and +2.0 boundaries across 2017 and 2021.

residualRisks: Depending entirely on bitview.space `cointime_value_stored_cumulative` introduces single-provider dependency; if bitview changes their calculation of this metric, the `0.105` alpha exponent will require recalibration.
childReport.diffSummary: No repository files changed; created research report detailing the Cointime Value Stored normalization multiplier for the Valuation system.
childReport.manualNotes: The research scripts used to identify this multiplier were written to temporary files and cleaned up after execution. The multiplier effectively aligns the 2017 top (~150), the April 2021 top (~178), and the Nov 2021 top (~150).
